title: Integrate container image test scripts into CI workflows
description:
**Size:** 📦 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Source:** Proposed for 'The tests under tests/container-images/ are currently'

1. Create `.github/workflows/verify-container-images.yaml` — a new single-job workflow triggered on push (main, stable/**) and pull_request that installs yq and runs 5 static test scripts sequentially: verify_build_images_workflow.sh, verify_deviation_comments.sh, verify_release_config.sh, verify_spdx_headers.sh, test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh. Follow existing conventions (SPDX header, SHA-pinned actions/checkout, permissions: contents: read, concurrency ${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }} with cancel-in-progress only on PR, timeout-minutes, runs-on: ubuntu-latest).

2. Modify `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` — add a verification step/job after build-base-images that runs verify_python_base.sh and verify_venv_builder.sh with digest-tagged image refs from job outputs. Replace the inline PR smoke test (~lines 191–196) and the push-only smoke-test job (~lines 198–254) with verify_keystone.sh; push path retains docker pull from GHCR before invocation, PR path uses local --load image.

3. Fix test script issues — add assert_not_empty to tests/lib/assertions.sh; refactor verify_keystone.sh manual pass/fail block (lines 31–37) to use it and align default image tag with CI tags; fix stale uv version in verify_venv_builder.sh (0.6.3 → match Dockerfile); evaluate/harden sed-based extras extraction in verify_release_config.sh (lines 97–108).

4. Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md to document test integration, verification coverage, and the new workflow.

**Rationale:** Eight test scripts exist but zero are executed in CI, meaning container image structure validation, SPDX compliance checks, release config validation, and Dockerfile convention enforcement provide no automated protection. Bugs like the stale uv version expectation in verify_venv_builder.sh (tests for 0.6.3 while Dockerfile uses 0.10.8) would fail immediately if CI ran the scripts, proving the gap is real. Integrating these scripts catches regressions at PR time, replaces the limited inline smoke test (single --version check) with comprehensive verification (non-root user, no build tools, runtime packages), and ensures the existing test investment delivers value.

**Affected Areas:**
- .github/workflows/verify-container-images.yaml
- .github/workflows/build-images.yaml
- tests/lib/assertions.sh
- tests/container-images/verify_keystone.sh
- tests/container-images/verify_venv_builder.sh
- tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh
- docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md
elaboration:
**Size:** 🔨 medium
**Category:** infrastructure
**Priority:** high
**Feature ID:** CC-0028

## Summary

Seven test scripts under `tests/container-images/` and one under `tests/scripts/` validate container image structure, Dockerfile conventions, release configuration, SPDX headers, the `build-images.yaml` workflow structure, and the constraint override script — but none are executed by any GitHub Actions workflow. This feature (CC-0028) integrates all scripts into CI via a new `verify-container-images.yaml` workflow (static tests) and modifications to `build-images.yaml` (Docker-based image tests), reviews and fixes known issues in the scripts, and updates documentation.

## Scope

**Included:**
- New workflow `.github/workflows/verify-container-images.yaml` running 5 static tests (`verify_build_images_workflow.sh`, `verify_deviation_comments.sh`, `verify_release_config.sh`, `verify_spdx_headers.sh`, `test_apply_constraint_overrides.sh`) on push to `main`/`stable/**` and on `pull_request`
- Integration of `verify_python_base.sh` and `verify_venv_builder.sh` into `build-images.yaml` as a new verification step after `build-base-images` completes, passing built image digest refs as arguments
- Integration of `verify_keystone.sh` into `build-images.yaml`, replacing **both** the inline PR smoke test (lines 191–196) and the push-only `smoke-test` job (lines 198–254). The push path retains `docker pull` from GHCR before invoking `verify_keystone.sh`, so registry availability is implicitly verified. The script provides strictly more coverage than the one-liner (`--version` check + non-root user + no build tools + runtime packages)
- Review and fix known issues in existing test scripts:
  - Add `assert_not_empty` to `tests/lib/assertions.sh` and refactor `verify_keystone.sh` manual pass/fail counting (lines 31–37) to use it
  - Fix stale `uv` version expectation in `verify_venv_builder.sh` (tests for `0.6.3`, Dockerfile copies from `uv:0.10.8`)
  - Evaluate fragile `sed`-based extras extraction in `verify_release_config.sh` (lines 97–108) for robustness
  - Ensure `verify_keystone.sh` default image tag aligns with CI-built tags (current default: `c5c3/keystone:28.0.0`)
- Install `yq` in CI runner environments where needed (the new `verify-container-images.yaml` requires it for `verify_build_images_workflow.sh` and `verify_release_config.sh`; `build-images.yaml` already has `yq` available on `ubuntu-latest`)
- Ensure new/modified workflow files follow existing conventions: SPDX headers, SHA-pinned actions with version comments, concurrency control (`${{ github.ref }}-${{ github.workflow }}`), least-privilege permissions, `timeout-minutes`, expression injection defense (matrix values via `env:`, not `${{ }}` in `run:`)
- Update `docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md` to document the test integration

**Excluded:**
- Writing entirely new test scripts beyond what exists (YAGNI — 8 scripts cover current requirements)
- Modifying Dockerfiles, release configuration, or `apply-constraint-overrides.sh` itself (separate concerns; CC-0027 domain)
- Path-based trigger filtering for workflows (optimization, not requested)
- Integration tests requiring Kubernetes or OpenStack services
- Modifying `ci.yaml` (unrelated Go CI domain)

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant CI as build-images.yaml
    participant Base as build-base-images
    participant VB as verify base images
    participant Svc as build-service-images
    participant VS as verify service image

    CI->>Base: Build python-base + venv-builder
    Base-->>VB: digest refs
    VB->>VB: verify_python_base.sh + verify_venv_builder.sh
    VB-->>Svc: pass
    Svc->>Svc: Build keystone image
    Svc-->>VS: image ref
    Note over VS: PR: local image via --load
    Note over VS: Push: docker pull from GHCR
    VS->>VS: verify_keystone.sh
```

## Key Components

- **`.github/workflows/verify-container-images.yaml` (new)**: Single-job workflow triggered on push/PR; installs `yq`; runs 5 static test scripts sequentially; follows existing conventions (SPDX header, SHA-pinned `actions/checkout`, concurrency, `permissions: contents: read`, `timeout-minutes`, `runs-on: ubuntu-latest`)
- **`.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` (modified)**: Adds a verification step/job after `build-base-images` that runs `verify_python_base.sh` and `verify_venv_builder.sh` with digest-tagged image refs from job outputs; replaces the inline PR smoke test and the push-only `smoke-test` job with `verify_keystone.sh` (push path retains `docker pull` before invocation)
- **`tests/lib/assertions.sh` (modified)**: Add `assert_not_empty` helper function matching existing conventions (increments `PASS`/`FAIL`, prints result)
- **`tests/container-images/verify_keystone.sh` (modified)**: Refactor manual pass/fail block to use `assert_not_empty`; ensure first argument is used as image ref without hardcoded default assumptions incompatible with CI tags
- **`tests/container-images/verify_venv_builder.sh` (modified)**: Fix stale uv version expectation (`0.6.3` → `0.10.8` to match Dockerfile's `ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:0.10.8`)
- **`tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh` (review)**: Evaluate and potentially harden the `sed`-based pip extras extraction (lines 97–108) against format changes
- **`docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md` (updated)**: Document which tests run where, verification coverage, and the new `verify-container-images.yaml` workflow
